story_bosses.name: Ratha (Corrupted)

phases.name: Normal State
phases.strategy: Focus on building kinship gauge. Use head-to-head opportunities to counter attack patterns.
phases.notes: Ratha follows standard Flying Wyvern patterns. Learn the timing for easy phase.

phases.name: Corrupted Awakening
phases.strategy: Corruption makes Ratha more aggressive. Wait for openings after big attacks. Use kinship attacks during topple windows only.
phases.notes: Most dangerous phase - prioritize survival over damage. Break wings to limit mobility.

phases.name: Final Desperation
phases.strategy: Survive the Nova attack by using environmental cover. Afterward, Ratha is vulnerable - finish with kinship attack.
phases.notes: Don't greed for damage during Nova charge. Environmental awareness is key to survival.

element_strategy.notes: Corruption makes Ratha weak to thunder. Bring thunder monsties for advantage.

tips: Stock up on healing items before fight
Practice head-to-head timing in earlier fights
Don't panic during corruption phase - play defensively
Save kinship attacks for topple opportunities
Environmental awareness is crucial for Nova attack

story_bosses.name: Versa Pietru

phases.name: Tactical Combat
phases.strategy: Versa fights with monster partners. Target his monsters first to limit his options, then focus him.
phases.notes: First rival boss with full team tactics. Teaches importance of target prioritization.

phases.name: Desperation Tactics
phases.strategy: Versa becomes reckless when low. Weather his berserker assault, then punish with kinship attacks.
phases.notes: Shows human bosses can be as dangerous as monsters when desperate.

recommended_team.reason: Hits multiple targets including Versa's team

element_strategy.notes: Adapt strategy based on Versa's current team composition. He changes monsters between attempts.

tips: Study Versa's team before engaging
Prioritize taking out his support monsters first
Save kinship gauge for his monster swaps
Adapt element coverage based on his current team
Don't get tunnel vision - watch all three enemy targets

elder_dragons.name: Kirin
elder_dragons.unlock_conditions: Complete main story, Thunder Ridge S-rank ecosystem

phases.name: Lightning Master
phases.strategy: Kirin is extremely mobile. Use prediction over reaction. Lightning attacks have tell-tale sparks before striking.
phases.notes: Most agile elder dragon. Requires perfect timing and positioning.

phases.name: Storm Lord
phases.strategy: Enraged Kirin covers entire battlefield with lightning. Team positioning critical. Focus fire during charging moments.
phases.notes: Ultimate test of lightning dodge timing. Learn the patterns or get one-shot.

recommended_team.reason: High HP to survive lightning, immune to paralysis

element_strategy.notes: Kirin is immune to thunder and paralysis. Fire works best due to his agility.

tips: Study lightning patterns - they're consistent once learned
Never stand still - constant movement is survival
Bring paralysis immunity or cleansing items
Attack during charge-up animations only
Team spread prevents chain lightning wipes

elder_dragons.name: Teostra
elder_dragons.unlock_conditions: Volcanic region S-rank, defeat Uragaan and Lavasioth

phases.name: Fire Emperor
phases.strategy: Teostra's aura does constant damage. Use ice monsters or cooling items. Interrupt nova charges at all costs.
phases.notes: Fire aura makes close combat difficult. Plan for sustained fire damage.

phases.name: Supernova
phases.strategy: Nova charges faster when Teostra is low. Coordinate team burst damage to interrupt or flee when charge begins.
phases.notes: Most dangerous elder dragon due to instant-kill potential. Never get greedy.

recommended_team.reason: Counters fire aura and does massive damage
Can blow away flame aura temporarily

element_strategy.notes: Ice is mandatory for surviving flame aura. Water also effective but less so than ice.

tips: Bring cooling drinks and ice element monsters mandatory
Never ignore nova build-up - drop everything to interrupt
Keep team spread to avoid multi-hits from aura
Stock up on fire resistance before attempting
Learn the nova timing - consistent audio/visual cues

elder_dragons.name: Kushala Daora
elder_dragons.unlock_conditions: Icy Peaks S-rank ecosystem, complete ice region storyline

phases.name: Wind Master

attack_patterns.name: Tornado
attack_patterns.counter: Stay in center eye or far range

phases.strategy: Kushala's wind barriers prevent approach. Use fire attacks to dispel barriers, then rush in for damage.
phases.notes: Most defensive elder dragon. Requires specific counter-strategies to approach.

phases.name: Storm Lord

attack_patterns.name: Hurricane
attack_patterns.counter: Seek shelter or use wind-immune monsters

phases.strategy: Enraged Kushala rarely lands. Focus on flying monsters or ranged attacks. Hurricane attack requires shelter.
phases.notes: Hardest elder dragon to approach. Fight becomes aerial chess match.

recommended_team.reason: Can fight Kushala in the air
Too heavy for wind attacks to move

element_strategy.notes: Fire dispels wind barriers and is essential. Thunder also effective for aerial hits.

tips: Fire element is mandatory for dispelling wind barriers
Bring flying monsters for aerial phase
Stock up on hot drinks for ice wind attacks
Heavy monsters resist wind pressure better
Learn hurricane shelter locations in each arena

elder_dragons.name: Nergigante
elder_dragons.unlock_conditions: Elder's Recess access, defeat all other elder dragons

phases.name: White Spikes
phases.strategy: Focus on breaking Nergigante's white spikes before they turn black. Black spikes enable dive bomb.
phases.notes: Spike management is key. White spikes are breakable, black spikes are not.

phases.name: Black Spikes

attack_patterns.name: Hardened Slam
attack_patterns.counter: Must dodge - cannot break black spikes

phases.strategy: Black spikes cannot be broken. Focus on dodging and sustained DPS. Prepare for dive bomb when he builds up.
phases.notes: Most dangerous phase - black spikes make Nergigante extremely dangerous.

phases.name: Desperation
phases.strategy: Final phase is pure survival. Dive bombs come constantly. Only attack during brief windows after missed dives.
phases.notes: Ultimate test of patience and timing. Many hunters get overconfident in final phase.

recommended_team.reason: Understands spike patterns, high raw damage
High raw damage for spike breaking

element_strategy.notes: Raw damage more important than element. Focus on high attack power.

tips: Break white spikes immediately - priority target
Never attack black spikes - waste of time and dangerous
Learn dive bomb dodge timing - practice makes perfect
Bring high raw damage weapons over elemental
Stock up on healing items - this is a marathon fight

elder_dragons.name: Fatalis
elder_dragons.unlock_conditions: Complete all elder dragons, post-game storyline, Castle Schrade access

phases.name: Legend Awakens
phases.strategy: Fatalis has no safe zones. Every attack can one-shot. Perfect positioning and timing mandatory for survival.
phases.notes: Legendary difficulty means exactly that. Only attempt with perfect preparation.

phases.name: Castle Destroyer
phases.strategy: Fatalis destroys the arena itself. Adapt to changing terrain while maintaining DPS under constant pressure.
phases.notes: Environmental mastery required. Fight becomes dynamic as arena changes.

phases.name: True Black Dragon
phases.strategy: Final phase transcends normal game mechanics. Every action must be perfect. No room for error.
phases.notes: Ultimate challenge. Only the most skilled hunters can hope to survive.

element_strategy.notes: Fatalis resists everything. Focus on highest possible raw damage and perfect technique.

rewards.special: Fatalis egg (impossible rarity), Castle Schrade residence

tips: Perfect your technique on all other elder dragons first
Bring maximum healing items and defensive skills
Learn every attack pattern frame-by-frame
Expect multiple attempts - this is the ultimate challenge
Master environmental awareness as arena changes
Only attempt with S-tier team and perfect builds

variant_bosses.name: Dreadqueen Rathian
variant_bosses.unlock_conditions: Rathian habitat S-rank + 3 Poison monsters in ecosystem

phases.name: Poison Queen
phases.strategy: Dreadqueen's poison is more potent than regular Rathian. Keep antidotes ready and interrupt Solar Cry to prevent kinship spam.
phases.notes: Evolved Rathian with enhanced poison and team support abilities.

phases.name: AoE Supremacy
phases.strategy: Enraged Dreadqueen has devastating AoE. Position carefully and race to finish before she can set up kill zones.
phases.notes: Most dangerous Flying Wyvern variant due to area control.

recommended_team.reason: Matches her power level and counters flying

element_strategy.notes: Poison immunity mandatory. Thunder works well due to technical type advantage.

tips: Poison immunity or constant antidote supply mandatory
Focus on interrupting Solar Cry charges
Learn AoE field patterns - positioning is survival
Bring highest DPS possible for phase 2 race
Technical type monsters have advantage over her Power moves

preparation: Bring appropriate element coverage for target monster
Stock up on healing items and status remedies
Practice head-to-head timing with easier monsters first
combat_tactics: Master head-to-head system - it's core to all fights
Save kinship attacks for topple opportunities only
Learn to read attack telegraphs for counter timing
Positioning beats pure damage - stay alive first
Adapt strategy based on monster behavior changes
team_building: Balanced teams outperform specialized in most fights
Match elements to monster weaknesses for 3-star damage
Consider monster mobility - flying vs ground matchups
Backup strategies for when primary plan fails
